parameters.idea: We want to build a mobile expense tracker app for small business owners...

functional_requirements.title: Receipt Photo Capture
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow users to photograph receipts using the device camera

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated as core feature — 'photograph receipts'

citation.source_reference: photograph receipts and the app automatically extracts

trajectory.detail: Identified receipt capture as primary input mechanism
Core user action — Must-Have priority
Formulated as camera-based capture requirement

functional_requirements.title: Automatic Receipt Data Extraction
functional_requirements.description: The system shall automatically extract amount, date, and vendor name from photographed receipts using OCR

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'automatically extracts the amount, date, and vendor'

citation.source_reference: automatically extracts the amount, date, and vendor

trajectory.detail: Three data points specified: amount, date, vendor
Automation is the core value prop — Must-Have
Added OCR as implied technology for extraction

functional_requirements.title: Expense Categorisation
functional_requirements.description: The system shall categorise expenses into predefined categories (travel, meals, office supplies, and others)

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated with example categories provided

citation.source_reference: categorise expenses (travel, meals, office supplies, etc.)

trajectory.detail: Categories listed: travel, meals, office supplies + etc.
Core feature, explicitly listed
Noted 'etc.' implies extensible category list

functional_requirements.title: Monthly Report Generation
functional_requirements.description: The system shall generate monthly expense reports summarising spending by category

metadata.reasoning: Stated as 'generate monthly reports' — important but not blocking core functionality

trajectory.detail: Monthly cadence specified
Reporting is value-add, core app works without it initially
Should-Have — delivers value but not day-1 critical

functional_requirements.title: Budget Setting Per Category
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow users to set spending budget limits per expense category

trajectory.detail: Budget + category link explicit
Paired with alerts — core budget feature
Must-Have as it enables the alert feature

functional_requirements.title: Budget Threshold Alerts
functional_requirements.description: The system shall notify users when spending approaches the configured budget limit for any category

metadata.reasoning: Explicitly stated — 'get alerts when they're close to the limit'

citation.source_reference: get alerts when they're close to the limit

trajectory.detail: Alerts tied to budget proximity
Proactive notification — core value for budget control
Must-Have — explicitly requested

non_functional_requirements.title: Offline Operation
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall function fully offline for expense capture and categorisation, and synchronise data when connectivity is restored

citation.source_reference: must work offline and sync when back online

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Explicitly stated with 'must' — non-negotiable. This is an architectural constraint that affects data storage and sync design.

trajectory.detail: Offline + sync explicitly required
'must work' = Must-Have. Affects architecture significantly.
Categorised as NFR-Reliability rather than FR as it's a quality attribute

constraints.reasoning: Fixed technology decision — constrains deployment and service choices. Not a requirement to build.
Fixed framework decision — constrains UI implementation. Implies cross-platform (iOS + Android) from single codebase.

assumptions.description: The app targets both iOS and Android since React Native was specified (cross-platform framework)
assumptions.reasoning: React Native implies both platforms, but not explicitly stated. Could be iOS-only with React Native for future portability.

assumptions.description: Expense categories are predefined by the system with the ability for users to add custom categories
assumptions.reasoning: Input says 'categorise' and lists examples with 'etc.' — implies predefined list but doesn't confirm if users can create custom categories.

gaps.description: No authentication or user account management specified
gaps.impact: Cannot design login, security, multi-device sync, or data privacy
gaps.suggested_question: How should users authenticate? Email/password, social login, biometric? Is multi-device access needed?
gaps.reasoning: App handles financial data but no auth approach mentioned. Critical for security and compliance.

gaps.description: No data retention or export requirements specified
gaps.impact: Cannot determine storage duration, compliance obligations, or export formats
gaps.suggested_question: How long should expense data be retained? Do users need CSV/PDF export? Any tax compliance requirements?
gaps.reasoning: Small business expense data likely has tax implications — retention and export are essential but unspecified.

output.execution_summary:
• Produced 6 FRs (5 Must-Have, 1 Should-Have), 1 NFR, 2 constraints, 2 assumptions, 2 gaps
• Key decisions: classified offline/sync as NFR-Reliability (not FR) since it's a quality attribute; AWS/React Native as constraints (not requirements) since they're fixed decisions
• Reflection: initially had FR-04 (reports) as Must-Have — downgraded to Should-Have since core app value is capture+categorise, not reporting. Added ASM-02 about custom categories which was initially missed.
• 2 gaps need stakeholder input before design: authentication approach and data retention/export requirements.
